summary: sensivity ships a FiveM game-cheat with Windows persistence, hardware fingerprint exfiltration, and obfuscated eval'd payload
details: The package's main entry launcher.js performs several harmful actions on load. (1) Persistence: it writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\OneDriveUpdate via PowerShell Set-ItemProperty, pointing wscript.exe at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that re-launches `node launcher.js` at every Windows login. The 'OneDriveUpdate' name is deliberate Microsoft-OneDrive impersonation. (2) Obfuscated remote-style execution: launcher.js calls `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js','utf8'))` on a 155 KB obfuscator.io-packed file (RC4 string array, control-flow flattening) — the consumer cannot inspect what actually runs. (3) Exfiltration: the eval'd server.obf.js builds a SHA-256 HWID from the Windows registry MachineGuid, os.hostname(), and the system volume serial (queried via PowerShell Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk), then POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app, version} to a license endpoint whose host string is fragmented across the obfuscated array (`'https://iz'+...+'eck.php'`). (4) Bundled native binary: sens.node is a 6.8 MB Windows addon containing FiveM/GTA V game-cheat strings ('Enable FL','Freecam','Teleport','Waypoint','spawnVehicle','License Accepted') and is loaded via require() and exposed through a local Socket.IO/Express server on port 3000. (5) Hostile to other dev tooling: launcher.js force-kills any process listening on TCP 3000 via `Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 3000... Stop-Process -Force`. The npm description 'Sensivity Control Panel' does not disclose any of this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
